category.category_description: Covers educational initiatives, learning resources, and skill development.

VERY_SHORT.string: Devbhoomi Divyang Jan Gurukul Offline Center Appeal

SHORT.string: Amit Singh Negi, along with students from various states, appeals for donations to build an offline center for visually impaired children. The center will provide daily living skills, computer training, hostel facilities, nutritious meals, and a museum with 2D, 3D, 4D, and 8D models to aid learning. They request viewers to scan the QR code on screen to contribute funds for the project.

MEDIUM.markdown_string:
# Building a Dream: Devbhoomi Divyang Jan Gurukul’s Offline Center Appeal

In a quiet corner of Dehradun, Uttarakhand, a vision is taking shape—one that promises to transform the lives of visually impaired children across India. Amit Singh Negi, the founder of Devbhoomi Divyang Jan Gurukul, stands on a plot of land that will soon become an offline center dedicated to empowering children who are blind or visually impaired. But this isn’t just a building project; it’s a movement, fueled by passion, community, and the unwavering belief that every child deserves a chance to see the world—not with their eyes, but with their minds.

## The Voices Behind the Vision

Amit is not alone in this endeavor. Standing beside him are students who have traveled from different states, united by a shared purpose. They introduce themselves with warmth and pride:

- **Dron** from Dehradun, Uttarakhand
- **Kamalpreet Jaku** from Punjab
- **Shivani Goyal** from Haryana
- **Gungun** from Sonipat, Haryana
- **Rishi Chaudhary** from Dehradun, Uttarakhand

These young people are not just supporters; they are living proof of the impact that such a center can have. They have come to this site to help build something that will change lives—and they are asking for your help.

## What the Offline Center Will Offer

This offline center is not just a building; it is a lifeline. Amit explains the vision with clarity and emotion:

> “Through this offline center, we will provide our visually impaired children with daily living skills, computer skills, hostel facilities, and nutritious food. We also plan to create a museum where, through 2D, 3D, 4D, and 8D models, we can help them visualize images in their minds—so that when they encounter a concept during their studies, they can understand it easily.”

This is not just education; it is empowerment. The center will address the holistic needs of visually impaired children, from basic life skills to advanced technological training. The museum, in particular, is a groundbreaking idea—using tactile and multi-dimensional models to bridge the gap between abstract concepts and tangible understanding.

## The Power of Community

Amit’s appeal is heartfelt and direct. He knows that this dream cannot be realized alone. He turns to the camera and speaks to every viewer:

> “We specially request all of you to scan the QR code on your screen and contribute your valuable donation in the form of money, so that we can build our offline center as soon as possible. Thank you. Let’s join hands together. Let’s make dreams come true.”

The message is simple but profound: *Milkar haath badhaiye, sapne sach banaiye*—Let’s join hands and make dreams come true.

## Why This Matters

In a country where disability often means exclusion, Devbhoomi Divyang Jan Gurukul is creating a space of inclusion, dignity, and opportunity. Visually impaired children are not just being taught; they are being equipped to navigate the world with confidence. The center will provide:

- **Daily Living Skills**: Teaching children how to manage everyday tasks independently.
- **Computer Skills**: Opening doors to technology and digital literacy.
- **Hostel Facilities**: A safe, supportive environment for learning and growth.
- **Nutritious Meals**: Ensuring physical well-being alongside education.
- **Innovative Museum**: Using multi-dimensional models to make learning accessible and engaging.

## A Call to Action

This is more than a fundraising appeal; it is an invitation to be part of something transformative. Every contribution, no matter how small, brings this center one step closer to reality. The QR code on the screen is not just a link—it is a bridge between a dream and its fulfillment.

As Amit and his students stand on that piece of land, they are not just building a center; they are building hope. And they are asking you to build it with them.

**Scan the QR code. Donate. Be part of the dream.**

*Devbhoomi Divyang Jan Gurukul – Where every child learns to see the world, one model at a time.*
